15	
16	def MapNestedForallToGpuThreadsOp :
17	  Op<Transform_Dialect, "iree.map_nested_forall_to_gpu_threads",
18	    [DeclareOpInterfaceMethods<MemoryEffectsOpInterface>,
19	     TransformEachOpTrait,
20	     TransformOpInterface,
21	     ReportTrackingListenerFailuresOpTrait]> {
22	  let description = [{
23	    Target the whole hal.executable_variant op and rewrite all scf.forall
24	    to distributed gpu.thread_id and translation_info attribute.
25	
26	    This op will handle all the scf.forall using gpu.thread or gpu.warp
27	    mapping.
28	
29	    The mapping of threads to gpu.thread_id is currently one-to-one and in order.
30	    Only **bufferized** scf.forall are currently supported.
31	    Only scf.forall distributed to **at most 3 dimensions** are currently
32	    supported.
33	
34	    Multiple scf.forall are supported per function in which case, the
35	    max of all the threads is computed and taken for the global gpu.thread_id.
36	    If necessary, scf.forall that do not use the whole thread range
37	    result in predicated computations.
38	
39	    Barriers are inserted after each scf.forall op
40	    if `sync_after_distribution` is true.
41	
42	    Return modes:
43	    =============
44	    This operation ignores non-Func ops and drops them in the return.
45	
46	    If any scf.forall with tensors is found, the transform definitely
47	    fails.
48	
49	    If all the scf.forall operations contained within the FuncOp
50	    referred to by the `target` operand lower to GPU properly, the
51	    transform succeeds. Otherwise the transform definitely fails.
52	
53	    The returned handle points to the same FuncOp operand, consuming it and
54	    producing a new SSA value to satisfy chaining and linearity of the IR
55	    properties.
56	
57	    Example:
58	    ========
59	
60	    ```
61	    hal.executable {
62	      hal.executable.variant {
63	        hal.executable.export {
64	          func @foo() {
65	            scf.forall (%i, %j) in (7, 9) {
66	              ... // body 1
67	            }
68	            scf.forall (%i) in (12) {
69	              ... // body 2
70	            }
71	          }
72	    ```
73	
74	    is translated to:
75	
76	    ```
77	    hal.executable {
78	      hal.executable.variant {
79	        hal.executable.export ... workgroup_size = [12 : index, 9 : index, 1 : index] {
80	          func @foo() {
81	            if (threadIdx.x < 7) {
82	              ... // body 1
83	            }
84	            if (threadIdx.y < 1) {
85	              ... // body 2
86	            }
87	          }
88	    ```
89	  }];
90	
91	  let arguments = (ins TransformHandleTypeInterface:$target,
92	                   DefaultValuedAttr<DenseI64ArrayAttr, "{}">:$workgroup_dims,
93	                   DefaultValuedOptionalAttr<I64Attr, "32">:$subgroup_size,
94	                   DefaultValuedOptionalAttr<BoolAttr, "true">:$sync_after_distribution);
95	  let results = (outs);
96	
97	  let assemblyFormat = [{
98	    $target
99	    `workgroup_dims` `=` $workgroup_dims
100	    (`subgroup_size` `=` $subgroup_size^)?
101	    (`sync_after_distribution` `=` $sync_after_distribution^)?
102	    attr-dict
103	    `:` functional-type($target, results)
104	  }];
105	  let cppNamespace = "mlir::iree_compiler::IREE::transform_dialect";
106	
107	  let extraClassDeclaration = [{
108	    ::mlir::DiagnosedSilenceableFailure applyToOne(
109	        ::mlir::transform::TransformRewriter &rewriter,
110	        ::mlir::FunctionOpInterface target,
111	        ::mlir::transform::ApplyToEachResultList &results,
112	        ::mlir::transform::TransformState &state);

116	def VectorToWarpExecuteOnLane0Op : Op<Transform_Dialect, "iree.vector.to_warp_execute_on_lane_0",
117	    [FunctionalStyleTransformOpTrait,
118	     MemoryEffectsOpInterface,
119	     TransformEachOpTrait,
120	     TransformOpInterface,
121	     ReportTrackingListenerFailuresOpTrait]> {
122	  let description = [{
123	    Given an scf.if target predicated by `if (threadIdx.x == 0)`, rewrite its
124	    body to vector.execute_on_lane_0 running ***on a single warp***.
125	
126	    The warp size is determined by the `warp_size` attribute (it is generally
127	    32 but we do not want to hardcode it).
128	
129	    This rewrite only applies if it can be determined from the IR (i.e. from
130	    the surrounding IREE::HAL::ExecutableExportOp) that the number of threads
131	    along the warp dimension is a multiple of the warp size. The transformation
132	    bails on non-perfect multiples of the warp size that would not properly
133	    distribute.
134	
135	    This is the first of two step towards apply vector distribution to a single
136	    warp.
137	
138	
139	    Return modes:
140	    =============
141	    This operation ignores non-scf::IfOp ops and drops them in the return.
142	
143	    If all the operations referred to by the `target` operand are properly
144	    properly, the transform succeeds. Otherwise the transform silently fails.
145	
146	    If the transform is anchored at a top-level that is not isolated from above,
147	    the transform definitely fails.
148	
149	    If the transform cannot find a proper HAL::ExecutableExportOp with a
150	    well-formed workgroup_size 3-entry attribute such that the threadIdx.x
151	    component is a multiple of warp_size, the transform silently fails.
152	    If the scf::ForOp predicate does not predicate on threadIdx.x == 0, the
153	    transform silently fails.
154	
155	    Otherwise the transformation succeeds and the returned handle points to the
156	    produced vector::WarpExecuteOnThread0Op.
157	
158	
159	    Example:
160	    ========
161	
162	    ```
163	    hal.executable.export public @foo ... { workgroup_size = [64: index, 1: index, 1: index] }
164	    builtin.module {
165	      func.func @foo() {
166	        %c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
167	        %c1 = arith.constant 1 : index
168	        %0 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(#pipeline_layout) binding(1) alignment(64) offset(%c0) : memref<128xf32>
169	        %1 = gpu.thread_id  x
170	        %2 = arith.cmpi ult, %1, %c1 : index
171	        scf.if %2 {
172	          %3 = arith.constant dense<1.0> : vector<128xf32>
173	          vector.transfer_write %3, %0[%c0] : vector<128xf32>, memref<128xf32>
174	        }
175	      }
176	    }
177	    ```
178	
179	    rewrites to:
180	
181	    ```
182	    hal.executable.export public @foo ... { workgroup_size = [64: index, 1: index, 1: index] }
183	    builtin.module {
184	      func.func @foo() {
185	        %c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
186	        %c4 = arith.constant 4 : index
187	        %c32 = arith.constant 32 : index
188	        %cst = arith.constant dense<1.000000e+00> : vector<128xf32>
189	        %0 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(#pipeline_layout) binding(1) alignment(64) offset(%c0) : memref<128xf32>
190	        %1 = gpu.thread_id  x
191	        %2 = arith.cmpi ult, %1, %c32 : index
192	        // Single-warp guard filters out threads 32-63.
193	        scf.if %2 {
194	          vector.warp_execute_on_lane_0(%1)[32] {
195	            %cst = arith.constant dense<1.000000e+00> : vector<128xf32>
196	            vector.transfer_write %cst, %0[%c0] : vector<128xf32>, memref<128xf32>
197	          }
198	        }
199	      }
200	    }
201	    ```
202	  }];
203	
204	  let arguments = (ins TransformHandleTypeInterface:$target,
205	                   DefaultValuedAttr<I64Attr, "{}">:$warp_size);
206	  let results = (outs TransformHandleTypeInterface:$result);
207	
208	  let assemblyFormat = "$target attr-dict `:` functional-type($target, $result)";
209	  let cppNamespace = "mlir::iree_compiler::IREE::transform_dialect";
210	
211	  let builders = [
212	    OpBuilder<(ins "Value":$target, "int64_t":$warpSize)>
213	  ];
214	  let extraClassDeclaration = [{
215	    ::mlir::DiagnosedSilenceableFailure applyToOne(
216	        ::mlir::transform::TransformRewriter &rewriter,
217	        ::mlir::scf::IfOp target,
218	        ::mlir::transform::ApplyToEachResultList &results,
219	        ::mlir::transform::TransformState &state);
220	  }];
221	}
222	
223	def VectorWarpDistributionOp : Op<Transform_Dialect, "iree.vector.warp_distribute",
224	    [DeclareOpInterfaceMethods<MemoryEffectsOpInterface>,
225	     TransformEachOpTrait,
226	     TransformOpInterface,
227	     ReportTrackingListenerFailuresOpTrait]> {
228	  let description = [{
229	    Given a vector.warp_execute_on_lane_0, apply the patterns to rewrite into
230	    distributed form with warp synchronization. This produces IR that runs
231	    ***on a single warp***.
232	
233	    IR that cannot be distributed will be predicated by `if (threadIdx.x == 0)`.
234	
235	    This is the second step of two for applying vector distribution to a single
236	    warp.
237	
238	
239	    Return modes:
240	    =============
241	    This operation applies a number of patterns to rewrite vector IR into
242	    distributed warp form. To apply these patterns, this operation must target
243	    an operation that is isolated from above, otherwise the transform definitely
244	    fails.
245	
246	    Patterns sets are applied in the following order:
247	      - applyMultiReductionLoweringPatterns
248	      - applyVectorTransferWriteDistribution
249	      - applyPropagateVectorDistribution
250	      - applyWarpExecuteOnLane0ToScf
251	
252	    If any of the pattern sets fail to apply, the transformation definitely
253	    fails.
254	
255	    Otherwise the transformation is successful and no result is returned.
256	
257	
258	    Example:
259	    ========
260	
261	    ```
262	    hal.executable.export public @foo ... { workgroup_size = [64: index, 1: index, 1: index] }
263	    builtin.module {
264	      func.func @foo() {
265	        %c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
266	        %c4 = arith.constant 4 : index
267	        %c32 = arith.constant 32 : index
268	        %cst = arith.constant dense<1.000000e+00> : vector<128xf32>
269	        %0 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(#pipeline_layout) binding(1) alignment(64) offset(%c0) : memref<128xf32>
270	        %1 = gpu.thread_id  x
271	        %2 = arith.cmpi ult, %1, %c32 : index
272	        // Single-warp guard filters out threads 32-63.
273	        scf.if %2 {
274	          vector.warp_execute_on_lane_0(%1)[32] {
275	            %cst = arith.constant dense<1.000000e+00> : vector<128xf32>
276	            vector.transfer_write %cst, %0[%c0] : vector<128xf32>, memref<128xf32>
277	          }
278	      }
279	      }
280	    }
281	    ```
282	
283	    distributes to:
284	
285	    ```
286	    hal.executable.export public @foo ... { workgroup_size = [64: index, 1: index, 1: index] }
287	    builtin.module {
288	      func.func @foo() {
289	        %c0 = arith.constant 0 : index
290	        %c4 = arith.constant 4 : index
291	        %c32 = arith.constant 32 : index
292	        %cst = arith.constant dense<1.000000e+00> : vector<128xf32>
293	        %0 = hal.interface.binding.subspan layout(#pipeline_layout) binding(1) alignment(64) offset(%c0) : memref<128xf32>
294	        %1 = gpu.thread_id  x
295	        %2 = arith.cmpi ult, %1, %c32 : index
296	        // Single-warp guard filters out threads 32-63.
297	        scf.if %2 {
298	          %3 = arith.cmpi eq, %1, %c0 : index
299	          %4 = memref.alloc() : memref<128xf32, 3>
300	          // Single-thread guard runs on thread 0 only.
301	          scf.if %3 {
302	            vector.store %cst, %4[%c0] : memref<128xf32, 3>, vector<128xf32>
303	          }
304	          %5 = arith.muli %1, %c4 : index
305	          %6 = vector.load %4[%5] : memref<128xf32, 3>, vector<4xf32>
306	          %7 = affine.apply #map()[%1]
307	          vector.transfer_write %6, %0[%7] {in_bounds = [true]} : vector<4xf32>, memref<128xf32>
308	        }
309	      }
310	    }
311	    ```

410	def PipelineSharedMemoryCopiesOp : Op<
411	    Transform_Dialect, "iree.pipeline_shared_memory_copies", [
412	      FunctionalStyleTransformOpTrait,
413	      MemoryEffectsOpInterface,
414	      TransformEachOpTrait,
415	      TransformOpInterface,
416	      ReportTrackingListenerFailuresOpTrait]> {
417	  let description = [{
418	    This applies software pipelining to a given scf.for loop. The pipelining
419	    strategy will look for a copy to shared memory and pipeline it to overlap
420	    it with the rest of the loop.
421	    It is user responsability to ensure that there are no dependency between
422	    `depth` iterations of the loop by using multi-buffering.
423	
424	    `depth` will indicate how many stages the software pipeline should have.
425	    `peel_epilogue` allows to force the epilogue to be peeled out instead of
426	    potentially using predicated operations for the epilogue phase.
427	
428	    #### Return modes
429	    This transform consumes the scf.for handle and produces a result handle
430	    which points to a) the new scf.for loop generated (success case) or b) the
431	    existing scf.for loop (failure case).
432	  }];

13	  transform.annotate %op "compilation_info" = %config : !transform.any_op, !transform.any_param
14	  // Add a dummy unit attribute to be sure that the tuning spec applied.
15	  // Otherwise it would be difficult to tell if the lowering config attribute
16	  // comes from our tuning spec or if the compiler heuristic happened to produce
17	  // the same config as this script.

22	transform.named_sequence @match_mmt_f16_f16_f32(%root: !transform.any_op {transform.readonly}) -> !transform.any_op {
23	  transform.match.operation_name %root ["linalg.generic"] : !transform.any_op
24	  // transform.print %root {name = "Generic"} : !transform.any_op
25	  %ins, %outs = transform.iree.match.cast_compatible_dag_from_root %root {
26	    ^bb0(%lhs: tensor<?x?xf16>, %rhs: tensor<?x?xf16>, %out: tensor<?x?xf32>):
27	    %7 = linalg.generic {indexing_maps = [affine_map<(d0, d1, d2) -> (d0, d2)>,
28	                                          affine_map<(d0, d1, d2) -> (d1, d2)>,
29	                                          affine_map<(d0, d1, d2) -> (d0, d1)>],
30	                          iterator_types = ["parallel", "parallel", "reduction"]}
31	        ins(%lhs, %rhs : tensor<?x?xf16>, tensor<?x?xf16>) outs(%out : tensor<?x?xf32>) {
32	      ^bb0(%in: f16, %in_0: f16, %acc: f32):
33	        %8 = arith.extf %in : f16 to f32
34	        %9 = arith.extf %in_0 : f16 to f32
35	        %10 = arith.mulf %8, %9 : f32
36	        %11 = arith.addf %acc, %10 : f32
37	        linalg.yield %11 : f32
38	      } -> tensor<?x?xf32>

## Summary of IREE-Specific Transform Dialect Operations and Extensions

Based on my thorough search across the IREE compiler source, here are the key findings:

### 1. IREE Transform Dialect Operation Files

The IREE transform dialect extensions are organized across multiple domains, located in:

**Primary TransformExtensions directories:**
- `/home/nick/work/AutoIREE/third-party/iree-src/compiler/src/iree/compiler/Codegen/LLVMGPU/TransformExtensions/LLVMGPUExtensionsOps.td` (GPU operations)
- `/home/nick/work/AutoIREE/third-party/iree-src/compiler/src/iree/compiler/Codegen/LLVMCPU/TransformExtensions/LLVMCPUExtensionsOps.td` (CPU operations - minimal)
- `/home/nick/work/AutoIREE/third-party/iree-src/compiler/src/iree/compiler/Codegen/Dialect/GPU/TransformExtensions/IREEGPUExtensionsOps.td` (GPU-specific ops)
- `/home/nick/work/AutoIREE/third-party/iree-src/compiler/src/iree/compiler/Codegen/Common/TransformExtensions/CommonExtensionsOps.td` (Common across targets)
- `/home/nick/work/AutoIREE/third-party/iree-src/compiler/src/iree/compiler/Preprocessing/TransformExtensions/PreprocessingExtensionsOps.td` (Preprocessing/matching)
- `/home/nick/work/AutoIREE/third-party/iree-src/compiler/src/iree/compiler/Dialect/Flow/TransformExtensions/FlowExtensionsOps.td` (Flow dialect)
- `/home/nick/work/AutoIREE/third-party/iree-src/compiler/src/iree/compiler/Dialect/LinalgExt/TransformExtensions/LinalgExtExtensionsOps.td` (LinalgExt ops)
- `/home/nick/work/AutoIREE/third-party/iree-src/compiler/src/iree/compiler/Dialect/Util/TransformOps/UtilTransformOps.td` (Utility ops)

### 2. LLVMGPU Transform Operations

**GPU-specific threading and distribution ops:**
- `iree.map_nested_forall_to_gpu_threads` - Maps scf.forall to gpu.thread_id with optional predicates. Supports up to 3D thread distribution with workgroup synchronization.
- `iree.vector.to_warp_execute_on_lane_0` - Converts scf.if predicates to vector.warp_execute_on_lane_0 for single-warp operations.
- `iree.vector.warp_distribute` - Applies vector distribution patterns to warp-level operations, including multi-reduction lowering, transfer write distribution, and warp synchronization.
- `iree.vector.vector_to_mma_conversion` - Converts vector.contract operations to MMA (matrix-multiply-accumulate) operations, targeting warp-level tensor cores. Supports both use_wmma and use_mma_sync.

**GPU memory and optimization ops:**
- `iree.promote_operands` - Promotes specified operands to higher-memory spaces, with optional allocation tracking.
- `iree.pipeline_shared_memory_copies` - Software pipelining for shared memory copies with configurable depth and epilogue peeling.
- `iree.synchronize_loop` - Inserts gpu.barrier after scf.for loops.
- `iree.create_async_groups` - Converts shared memory copies to async copies with group management.
- `iree.reorder_transpose` - Moves transpose operations before elementwise operations to improve cache locality.
- `iree.eliminate_gpu_barriers` - Removes unnecessary GPU barriers based on memory effects analysis.
- `iree.pack_shared_memory_alloc` - Packs overlapping shared memory allocations to reduce memory usage.
- `iree.prefetch_shared_memory_copies` - Prefetches shared memory copies within loops.

**AMDGPU-specific ops:**
- `iree.amdgpu_distribute_vectors` - AMDGPU vector contraction distribution producing MFMA (Matrix Fused Multiply-Add) ops.
- `iree.create_matmul_mfma_tile_sizes` - Generates tile size parameters based on matmul dimensions for MFMA configurations.

### 3. Common Transform Extensions

**Pattern application ops:**
- `apply_patterns.iree.bubble_collapse` - Folds tensor.expand_shape with generic operations by collapsing dimensions.
- `apply_patterns.iree.bubble_expand` - Expands dimensions in generic operations.
- `apply_patterns.iree.bubble_pack_unpack` - Bubbles data layout operations (pack/unpack) across other ops.
- `apply_patterns.iree.fold_fill_into_pad` - Folds linalg.fill into tensor.pad when constants match.
- `apply_patterns.iree.fold_reshape_into_tensor_hal_interface` - Folds tensor reshapes into HAL interface operations.

### 8. Tuning Specifications and Codegen Strategies

**Key files:**
- `/home/nick/work/AutoIREE/third-party/iree-src/compiler/plugins/target/ROCM/builtins/tuning/iree_default_tuning_spec_gfx942.mlir` - Default AMD GFX942 tuning spec
- `/home/nick/work/AutoIREE/third-party/iree-src/compiler/src/iree/compiler/Codegen/Common/test/tuning_spec.mlir` - Tuning spec examples

**Tuning spec structure:**
- Uses `transform.named_sequence` with `iree_codegen.tuning_spec_entrypoint` attribute
- Includes pattern matching sequences (`@match_*`) and configuration application (`@apply_op_config`)
- Each kernel config specifies:
  - **lowering_config** with:
    - `workgroup` - tile sizes for workgroup level
    - `reduction` - tile sizes for reduction dimension
    - `thread` - tile sizes for thread level
    - `promote_operands` - operand indices to promote
    - `mma_kind` - matrix multiply-accumulate type (e.g., MFMA_F32_16x16x16_F16)
    - `subgroup_m_count`/`subgroup_n_count` - subgroup distribution
  - **translation_info** with:
    - `pipeline` - codegen strategy (LLVMGPUTileAndFuse, LLVMGPUVectorDistribute, etc.)
    - `workgroup_size` - GPU workgroup dimensions
    - `subgroup_size` - GPU subgroup size (typically 32 or 64)
    - `gpu_pipeline_options` - advanced options like prefetch_shared_memory

**From IREECodegenAttrs.td, available pipelines include:**
- **LLVMGPUTileAndFuse** (112) - Greedy tile + fuse distributed to threads
- LLVMGPUDefault, LLVMGPUBaseLowering, LLVMGPUDistribute, LLVMGPUVectorize
- LLVMGPUMatmulTensorCore, LLVMGPUMatmulTensorCoreMmaSync
- LLVMGPUTransposeSharedMem, LLVMGPUWarpReduction, LLVMGPUPackUnPack

- TransformDialectCodegen - Custom transform dialect specification
- Custom - Out-of-tree pipelines

### 10. Transform Dialect Script Example Pattern

Transform scripts follow this pattern:
```mlir
module attributes { transform.with_named_sequence } {
  transform.named_sequence @match_pattern(%op: !transform.any_op) 
    -> (!transform.any_op, !transform.any_param) {
    // Pattern matching logic
    // Yield matched operation and config
  }
  
  transform.named_sequence @apply_op_config(
      %op: !transform.any_op, %config: !transform.any_param) {
    transform.annotate %op "compilation_info" = %config
    transform.yield
  }
  
  transform.named_sequence @__kernel_config(
      %variant_op: !transform.any_op)
    attributes { iree_codegen.tuning_spec_entrypoint } {
    %res = transform.foreach_match in %variant_op
      @match_pattern -> @apply_op_config
    transform.yield %res
  }
}
```

### 11. Relationship Between Tuning Specs and Transform Scripts

1. **Tuning specs** define kernel-specific lowering configurations (tile sizes, MMA kinds, pipeline options)
2. **Transform scripts** provide matching logic to identify which kernels need which configurations
3. **Lowering configs** specify tiling parameters at multiple levels (workgroup, thread, reduction)
4. **Translation info** specifies which codegen pipeline to use and workgroup geometry
5. The `iree.match.cast_compatible_dag_from_root` operation enables pattern matching of complex operation DAGs with type compatibility
6. Configuration is applied via `transform.annotate` which attaches `compilation_info` attributes to operations

This architecture allows IREE to support both automatic heuristic-based configurations and user-provided tuning specifications via the transform dialect, enabling performance optimization for diverse GPU architectures and kernel patterns.
